title: Delicate Japanese Pul Biber Fried Rice
description: A funky, pillowy, comforting Japanese dish featuring pul biber, this plant-based-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner. It combines the flavors of Japan with the spice of pul biber.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the frozen peas and carrots and cook until thawed, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the pul biber and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked Japanese rice to the skillet or wok, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice mixture for about 5 minutes, until everything is well combined and heated through.
Add the soy sauce, sesame oil, and salt, and stir-fry for an additional minute.
Transfer the fried rice to a serving platter and garnish with chopped scallions.
